OS Basics

Subpage of Operating Systems

Diving deep into the design and structure of Operating Systems.

Phase 1 — Basics (Weeks 1–4). This is the most important phase. Every subsequent topic assumes fluency here. Focus on the process lifecycle, system calls, and the kernel/user boundary. Your computer organization background will make interrupts and privileged instructions click quickly — lean into that.

As you go along, you need to finish the following topics:

⚠️
Make a page for each topic as you go along and then delete this page once you are done.
OS History and Evolution
System Calls and APIs
Kernel vs User Space
Monolithic vs Microkernel vs Exokernel
Interrupts and Traps
Processes vs Threads — Overview
The Process Lifecycle
CPU Scheduling — Policies and Algorithms
Context Switching
I/O and Device Management
Privileged Instructions and Protection Rings
The Boot Proces
